revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 上侍塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に後方部、右奥に前方部） |別名 = 車塚 |所属 = |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[大田原市]]湯津上 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 21.05 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 7|経度秒 = 29.45 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長114m
高さ11.5m（後方部） |埋葬施設 = [[粘土槨]] |出土品 = [[銅鏡]]・[[管玉]]・石釧・鉄製品・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「[[侍塚古墳]]」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 前方後方墳としては栃木県第2位の規模 |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後方墳-南 |ラベル = 上侍塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''上侍塚古墳'''（かみさむらいづかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[大田原市]]湯津上（ゆづかみ）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[下侍塚古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」）。 下侍塚古墳とともに[[江戸時代]]に[[徳川光圀]]（[[水戸黄門]]）の命で日本で最初の学術的[[発掘調査]]が行われた古墳として知られる。本項では、上侍塚古墳の北にある'''[[#上侍塚北古墳|上侍塚北古墳]]'''についても解説する。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+那須地方の前方後方墳6基 !colspan=3|古墳群 !古墳名 !規模 !前方部 !葺石 !埋葬施設 |- |rowspan=3 colspan=3| ||[[下侍塚古墳]]||84m||南||有||木棺直葬 |- |'''上侍塚古墳'''||114m||南||有||[[粘土槨]] |- |'''上侍塚北古墳'''||48.5m||南|| ||不明 |- |rowspan=3|[[那須小川古墳群|那須
小川]]||colspan=2| ||[[駒形大塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||無||木炭槨 |- |rowspan=2|吉田
新宿||温泉
神社||[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||[[那須八幡塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された大型[[前方後方墳]]である。[[江戸時代]]の[[元禄]]5年（[[1692年]]）の発掘調査のほか、[[2021年]]度（[[令和]]3年度）から発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘長は114メートルを測り、[[那須地方]]の前方後方墳6基のうちでは最大規模、栃木県の前方後方墳としては[[藤本観音山古墳]]（[[足利市]]）に次ぐ第2位の規模になる。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められる{{Sfn|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また墳丘周囲では、墳丘の北・西・南側で幅約20メートルの周溝が認められる。埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における[[粘土槨]]と見られる{{Sfn|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[副葬品]]として、江戸時代の調査では[[銅鏡]]（[[捩文鏡]]か1）・[[管玉]]2・石釧1・鉄製品（鉄鏃18・甲冑片5・鉄器片1・鉄鉾身1・鎧板1・鎧片22・鉄刀片1）・[[土師器]]（[[高坏]]）が検出されている{{Sfn|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末葉頃と推定される。下侍塚古墳とともに那須地方を代表する古墳の1つであるとともに、江戸時代の調査後には下侍塚古墳と合わせて墳丘保存のための松が植えられていることから、日本で最初の学術的発掘調査かつ調査後の文化財保護の点で考古学史上においても重要視される古墳である。 古墳域は[[1951年]]（[[昭和]]26年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]、[[元禄]]5年（[[1692年]]）に[[徳川光圀]]の命で[[佐々宗淳]]・大金重貞による発掘調査。副葬品多数出土（『湯津神村車塚御修理』として記録、調査後は松板箱に入れて埋戻し）。 * [[1951年]]（昭和26年）6月9日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」のうち）。 * [[2021年]]度（[[令和]]3年度）、墳丘の地中レーダー探査（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団）。 * 2021年度（令和3年度）以降、墳丘周囲の発掘調査（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}{{small|左に後方部、右に前方部（2021年度発掘調査時）。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り史跡説明板。[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ 侍塚古墳]（大田原市ホームページ）。。 * 墳丘長：114メートル（または112メートル{{Sfn|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}） * 後方部 ** 長さ：60.5メートル ** 幅：58メートル ** 高さ：11.5メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：53.5メートル ** 幅：52メートル ** 高さ：6.5メートル 墳丘の北・西・南側では、幅約20メートルの周溝が確認されている。東側では硬い[[砂礫]]層まで掘り下げた状態が台地の端まで続いており、周溝は認められていない「[https://www.asahi.com/articles/DA3S15550871.html 栃木の上侍塚古墳：徳川光圀以来の調査 木箱の行方は／遺物入れ埋め戻された記述 レーダーでも見つからず]」『[[朝日新聞]]』夕刊2023年2月8日3面（同日閲覧）。 File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む == 出土品 == 江戸時代の調査で出土した副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[銅鏡]] 1 - 捩文鏡か。 * [[管玉]] 2 * 石釧 1 * 鉄製品 ** 鉄鏃 18 ** 甲冑片 5 ** 鉄器片 1 ** 鉄鉾身 1 ** 鎧板 1 ** 鎧片 22 ** 鉄刀片 1 * [[土師器]] - [[高坏]]。 記録によれば、調査後に出土品は松板箱に入れて8[[尺]]（約2.4メートル）下に埋めたとされる。ただし近年の[[地中レーダー探査]]では、箱に該当する応答は認められていない。 == 上侍塚北古墳 == [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kita Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|上侍塚北古墳}}{{small|左に後方部、右に前方部。}}]] '''上侍塚北古墳'''（かみさむらいづかきたこふん）は、上侍塚古墳の北にある古墳。形状は前方後方墳。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘長48.5メートルを測るが、墳丘は現在までに大きく削平を受けている。埋葬施設は明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 侍塚古墳 - 上侍塚古墳・下侍塚古墳を包括。1951年（昭和26年）6月9日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|483|侍塚古墳}}。 == 関連施設 == * 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館（大田原市湯津上） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（大田原市教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=斎藤忠|authorlink=斎藤忠|chapter=侍塚古墳|year=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|侍塚古墳（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大金宣亮|year=|chapter=侍塚古墳|title=[[日本大百科全書]]（ニッポニカ）|publisher=[[小学館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|侍塚古墳（日本大百科全書）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=上侍塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|上侍塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=橋本澄朗「上侍塚北古墳」|ref={{Harvid|上侍塚北古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=橋本澄朗「上侍塚古墳」|ref={{Harvid|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 侍塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|侍塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=眞保昌弘|year=2008|chapter=|title=侍塚古墳と那須国造碑 -下野の前方後方墳と古代石碑-（日本の遺跡25）|publisher=[[同成社]]|isbn=9784886214164|ref=}} * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 「上侍塚古墳 現地説明会資料」]（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター、2021年）。 - リンクは[[奈良文化財研究所]]「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115807 「上侍塚古墳 発掘調査速報」]（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター、2022年）。 - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/130361 「上侍塚古墳 第1回現地説明会資料」]（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター、2022年）。 - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/130551 「上侍塚古墳 第2回現地説明会資料」]（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター、2023年）。 - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/130837 「上侍塚古墳 第3回現地説明会資料」]（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター、2023年）。 - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[侍塚古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|483|侍塚古墳}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ 侍塚古墳] - 大田原市ホームページ {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=上侍塚北古墳 |1-1=栃木県の古墳 |1-2=大田原市の歴史 |1-3=前方後方墳 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみさむらいつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:大田原市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 上侍塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に後方部、右奥に前方部） |別名 = 車塚 |所属 = |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[大田原市]]湯津上 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 21.05 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 7|経度秒 = 29.45 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[前方後方墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長114m
高さ11.5m（後方部） |埋葬施設 = [[粘土槨]] |出土品 = [[銅鏡]]・[[管玉]]・石釧・鉄製品・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「[[侍塚古墳]]」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 前方後方墳としては栃木県第2位の規模 |地図 = Japan Tochigi |アイコン = 前方後方墳-南 |ラベル = 上侍塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|46|40|N|140|7|22|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|那須地方の前方後方墳分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|48|46.75|N|140|7|22.35|E}} | label1=下侍塚 | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size1=20 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|48|21.05|N|140|7|29.45|E}} | label2=上侍塚 | label-pos2=bottom | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size2=20 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|48|24.97|N|140|7|28.70|E}} | label3=上侍塚北 | label-pos3=left | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size3=20 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|45|40.62|N|140|7|15.42|E}} | label4=駒形大塚 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size4=20 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|45|0.25|N|140|8|2.88|E}} | label5=吉田温泉神社 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=前方後方墳-南.png | mark-size5=20 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|44|42.93|N|140|8|9.05|E}} | label6=那須八幡塚 | label-pos6=left | label-color6=black | mark6=前方後方墳-西.png | mark-size6=20 }} '''上侍塚古墳'''（かみさむらいづかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[大田原市]]湯津上（ゆづかみ）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[下侍塚古墳]]と合わせて国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」）。 下侍塚古墳とともに[[江戸時代]]に[[徳川光圀]]（[[水戸黄門]]）の命で日本で最初の学術的[[発掘調査]]が行われた古墳として知られる。本項では、上侍塚古墳の北にある'''[[#上侍塚北古墳|上侍塚北古墳]]'''についても解説する。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+那須地方の前方後方墳6基 !colspan=3|古墳群 !古墳名 !規模 !前方部 !葺石 !埋葬施設 |- |rowspan=3 colspan=3| ||[[下侍塚古墳]]||84m||南||有||木棺直葬 |- |'''上侍塚古墳'''||114m||南||有||[[粘土槨]] |- |'''上侍塚北古墳'''||48.5m||南|| ||不明 |- |rowspan=3|[[那須小川古墳群|那須
小川]]||colspan=2| ||[[駒形大塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||無||木炭槨 |- |rowspan=2|吉田
新宿||温泉
神社||[[吉田温泉神社古墳]]||50m||南||無||木炭槨 |- |八幡塚||[[那須八幡塚古墳]]||60.5m||西||有||木棺直葬 |}
栃木県北東部、[[那珂川]]右岸の段丘上に築造された大型[[前方後方墳]]である。[[江戸時代]]の[[元禄]]5年（[[1692年]]）の発掘調査のほか、[[2021年]]度（[[令和]]3年度）から発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘長は114メートルを測り、[[那須地方]]の前方後方墳6基のうちでは最大規模、栃木県の前方後方墳としては[[藤本観音山古墳]]（[[足利市]]）に次ぐ第2位の規模になる。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]が認められる{{Sfn|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。また墳丘周囲では、墳丘の北・西・南側で幅約20メートルの周溝が認められる。埋葬施設は後方部墳頂における[[粘土槨]]と見られる{{Sfn|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[副葬品]]として、江戸時代の調査では[[銅鏡]]（[[捩文鏡]]か1）・[[管玉]]2・石釧1・鉄製品（鉄鏃18・甲冑片5・鉄器片1・鉄鉾身1・鎧板1・鎧片22・鉄刀片1）・[[土師器]]（[[高坏]]）が検出されている{{Sfn|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期初頭の[[4世紀]]末葉頃と推定される。下侍塚古墳とともに那須地方を代表する古墳の1つであるとともに、江戸時代の調査後には下侍塚古墳と合わせて墳丘保存のための松が植えられていることから、日本で最初の学術的発掘調査かつ調査後の文化財保護の点で考古学史上においても重要視される古墳である。 古墳域は[[1951年]]（[[昭和]]26年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]、[[元禄]]5年（[[1692年]]）に[[徳川光圀]]の命で[[佐々宗淳]]・大金重貞による発掘調査。副葬品多数出土（『湯津神村車塚御修理』として記録、調査後は松板箱に入れて埋戻し）。 * [[1951年]]（昭和26年）6月9日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「[[侍塚古墳]]」のうち）。 * [[2021年]]度（[[令和]]3年度）、墳丘の地中レーダー探査（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団）。 * 2021年度（令和3年度）以降、墳丘周囲の発掘調査（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-4.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}{{small|左に後方部、右に前方部（2021年度発掘調査時）。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り史跡説明板。[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ 侍塚古墳]（大田原市ホームページ）。。 * 墳丘長：114メートル（または112メートル{{Sfn|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}） * 後方部 ** 長さ：60.5メートル ** 幅：58メートル ** 高さ：11.5メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：53.5メートル ** 幅：52メートル ** 高さ：6.5メートル 墳丘の北・西・南側では、幅約20メートルの周溝が確認されている。東側では硬い[[砂礫]]層まで掘り下げた状態が台地の端まで続いており、周溝は認められていない「[https://www.asahi.com/articles/DA3S15550871.html 栃木の上侍塚古墳：徳川光圀以来の調査 木箱の行方は／遺物入れ埋め戻された記述 レーダーでも見つからず]」『[[朝日新聞]]』夕刊2023年2月8日3面（同日閲覧）。 File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|後方部墳頂 File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|前方部から後方部を望む File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後方部から前方部を望む == 出土品 == 江戸時代の調査で出土した副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[銅鏡]] 1 - 捩文鏡か。 * [[管玉]] 2 * 石釧 1 * 鉄製品 ** 鉄鏃 18 ** 甲冑片 5 ** 鉄器片 1 ** 鉄鉾身 1 ** 鎧板 1 ** 鎧片 22 ** 鉄刀片 1 * [[土師器]] - [[高坏]]。 記録によれば、調査後に出土品は松板箱に入れて8[[尺]]（約2.4メートル）下に埋めたとされる。ただし近年の[[地中レーダー探査]]では、箱に該当する応答は認められていない。 == 上侍塚北古墳 == [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kita Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|上侍塚北古墳}}{{small|左に後方部、右に前方部。}}]] '''上侍塚北古墳'''（かみさむらいづかきたこふん）は、上侍塚古墳の北にある古墳。形状は前方後方墳。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は前方後方形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘長48.5メートルを測るが、墳丘は現在までに大きく削平を受けている。埋葬施設は明らかでなく、副葬品も詳らかでない。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 侍塚古墳 - 上侍塚古墳・下侍塚古墳を包括。1951年（昭和26年）6月9日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|483|侍塚古墳}}。 == 関連施設 == * 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館（大田原市湯津上） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（大田原市教育委員会設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2013|chapter=|title=なす風土記ものがたり -なすの古墳をめぐって-（平成24年度 第1回企画展 大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館）|publisher=大田原市なす風土記の丘湯津上資料館|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|なす風土記ものがたり|2013}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|author=斎藤忠|authorlink=斎藤忠|chapter=侍塚古墳|year=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|侍塚古墳（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大金宣亮|year=|chapter=侍塚古墳|title=[[日本大百科全書]]（ニッポニカ）|publisher=[[小学館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|侍塚古墳（日本大百科全書）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=上侍塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 9 栃木県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490093|ref={{Harvid|上侍塚古墳（平凡社）|1988}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=橋本澄朗「上侍塚北古墳」|ref={{Harvid|上侍塚北古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=橋本澄朗「上侍塚古墳」|ref={{Harvid|上侍塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 侍塚古墳]|title=国指定史跡ガイド|publisher=[[講談社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|侍塚古墳（国指定史跡）}}}} - リンクは朝日新聞社「コトバンク」。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=眞保昌弘|year=2008|chapter=|title=侍塚古墳と那須国造碑 -下野の前方後方墳と古代石碑-（日本の遺跡25）|publisher=[[同成社]]|isbn=9784886214164|ref=}} * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 「上侍塚古墳 現地説明会資料」]（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター、2021年）。 - リンクは[[奈良文化財研究所]]「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115807 「上侍塚古墳 発掘調査速報」]（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター、2022年）。 - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/130361 「上侍塚古墳 第1回現地説明会資料」]（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター、2022年）。 - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/130551 「上侍塚古墳 第2回現地説明会資料」]（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター、2023年）。 - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/130837 「上侍塚古墳 第3回現地説明会資料」]（公益財団法人とちぎ未来づくり財団埋蔵文化財センター、2023年）。 - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[侍塚古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kamisamuraizuka Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|483|侍塚古墳}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ 侍塚古墳] - 大田原市ホームページ {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=上侍塚北古墳 |1-1=栃木県の古墳 |1-2=大田原市の歴史 |1-3=前方後方墳 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:かみさむらいつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:大田原市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:栃木県にある国指定の史跡]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound (left: posterior section, right: anterior section) |alternate_name = Kurumazuka | part_of = |location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|36.805861|N|140.124722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 114m, height: 11.5m (posterior section) |burial = Clay chamber |burial goods = Bronze mirror, tubular beads, stone armlets, iron objects, haji pottery |Subordinate Tombs = |built = Late 4th century |Buried Person = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Included in the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun" |Cultural Asset designation1 = |notes = Second largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in Tochigi Prefecture |map = Japan Tochigi |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-S |map_dot_label = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]]. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the [[:en:Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]].<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.

== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.

Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]]. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the [[:en:Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]].<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.
== External Links == * {{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] - Ōtawara City website.

'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]]. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the [[:en:Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]].<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.
== External Links == * {{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] - Ōtawara City website.
{{Kofun navbar}}

'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]]. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the [[:en:Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]].<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.
== External Links == * {{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] - Ōtawara City website.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound (left: posterior section, right: anterior section) |alternate_name = Kurumazuka | part_of = |location = [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|36.805861|N|140.124722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 114m, height: 11.5m (posterior section) |burial = Clay chamber |burial goods = Bronze mirror, tubular beads, stone armlets, iron objects, haji pottery |Subordinate Tombs = |built = Late 4th century |Buried Person = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Included in the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun" |Cultural Asset designation1 = |notes = Second largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in Tochigi Prefecture |map = Japan Tochigi |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-S |map_dot_label = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]]. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the [[:en:Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]].<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.
== External Links == * {{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] - Ōtawara City website.
{{Kofun navbar}}

'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]]. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the [[:en:Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]].<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.

The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.

== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.

'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]]. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the [[:en:Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]].<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.
== External Links == * {{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] - Ōtawara City website.

Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the [[:en:Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]].<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.

'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Ōtawara|Ōtawara]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]]. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the [[:en:Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Ashikaga, Tochigi|Ashikaga]].<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.
== External Links == * {{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] - Ōtawara City website.
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound (left: posterior section, right: anterior section) |alternate_name = Kurumazuka | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|WD=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|36.805861|N|140.124722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 114m, height: 11.5m (posterior section) |burial = Clay chamber |burial goods = Bronze mirror, tubular beads, stone armlets, iron objects, haji pottery |Subordinate Tombs = |built = Late 4th century |Buried Person = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Included in the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun" |Cultural Asset designation1 = |notes = Second largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in Tochigi Prefecture |map = Japan Tochigi |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-S |map_dot_label = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|WD=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}, known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the {{ill|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|en|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|WD=Q1076983|lt=Naka River}}. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|en|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|WD=Q11624242|lt=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga, Tochigi|WD=Q374477|lt=Ashikaga}}.<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.
== External Links == * {{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] - Ōtawara City website.
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound (left: posterior section, right: anterior section) |alternate_name = Kurumazuka | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|WD=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara|1=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|36.805861|N|140.124722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 114m, height: 11.5m (posterior section) |burial = Clay chamber |burial goods = Bronze mirror, tubular beads, stone armlets, iron objects, haji pottery |Subordinate Tombs = |built = Late 4th century |Buried Person = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Included in the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun" |Cultural Asset designation1 = |notes = Second largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in Tochigi Prefecture |map = Japan Tochigi |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-S |map_dot_label = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|WD=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara|1=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni|1=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}, known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the {{ill|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|en|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|WD=Q1076983|lt=Naka River|1=Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)}}. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|en|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|WD=Q11624242|lt=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|1=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga, Tochigi|WD=Q374477|lt=Ashikaga|1=Ashikaga, Tochigi}}.<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.
== External Links == * {{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] - Ōtawara City website.
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound (left: posterior section, right: anterior section) |alternate_name = Kurumazuka | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|WD=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara|1=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|36.805861|N|140.124722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 114m, height: 11.5m (posterior section) |burial = Clay chamber |burial goods = Bronze mirror, tubular beads, stone armlets, iron objects, haji pottery |Subordinate Tombs = |built = Late 4th century |Buried Person = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Included in the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun" |Cultural Asset designation1 = |notes = Second largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in Tochigi Prefecture |map = Japan Tochigi |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-S |map_dot_label = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|WD=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara|1=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni|1=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}, known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the {{ill|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|en|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|WD=Q1076983|lt=Naka River|1=Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)}}. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|en|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|WD=Q11624242|lt=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|1=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga, Tochigi|WD=Q374477|lt=Ashikaga|1=Ashikaga, Tochigi}}.<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.
== External Links == * {{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] - Ōtawara City website.
{{Kofun navbar}}
