{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |image = [[File:Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]] |caption = Mound (left: posterior section, right: anterior section) |alternate_name = Kurumazuka | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|qid=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara|1=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|36.805861|N|140.124722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Mound length: 114m, height: 11.5m (posterior section) |burial = Clay chamber |burial goods = Bronze mirror, tubular beads, stone armlets, iron objects, haji pottery |Subordinate Tombs = |built = Late 4th century |Buried Person = |Mausoleum = |alternate_name = Included in the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun" |Cultural Asset designation1 = |notes = Second largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in Tochigi Prefecture |map = Japan Tochigi |type = Zenpō-kōhō-fun-S |map_dot_label = Kamisamuraizuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }}
'''Kamisamuraizuka Kofun''' (上侍塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Ōtawara|en|Ōtawara|qid=Q386322|lt=Ōtawara|1=Ōtawara}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. Together with {{ill|Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimousamurai Kofun|lt=Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|ja|下侍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosamuraizuka Kofun|qq=}}, it is part of the National Historic Site "Samuraizuka Kofun." This kofun is notable for being one of the first to undergo a scholarly excavation during the Edo period under the orders of {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|qid=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni|1=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}, known as "Mito Kōmon."
== Overview == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun is a large zenpō-kōhō-fun constructed on the right bank of the {{ill|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|en|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|qid=Q1076983|lt=Naka River|1=Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)}}. Alongside Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, it represents one of the most significant ancient Kofuns in the Nasu region. Excavations date back to the 5th year of the Genroku era (1692), and further archaeological surveys were conducted starting in 2021.
The mound faces south, with a total length of 114 meters, making it the largest zenpō-kōhō-fun in the Nasu area and the second largest in Tochigi Prefecture after the {{ill|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|en|Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|qid=Q11624242|lt=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun|1=Fujimoto Kannonyama Kofun}} in {{ill|Ashikaga, Tochigi|en|Ashikaga, Tochigi|qid=Q374477|lt=Ashikaga|1=Ashikaga, Tochigi}}.<ref name="OhtawaraCity">[https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] (Ōtawara City website).</ref> The mound is covered with fukiishi (paving stones) and surrounded by a moat approximately 20 meters wide. The burial chamber is believed to have been constructed with clay.<ref name="descriptionboard">Site explanation board.</ref>
burial goods recovered during the Edo period include bronze mirrors, tubular beads, stone armlets, and iron objects such as armor fragments. These items were reburied in a wooden box following the excavation.<ref name="Asahi20230208">"Rediscovery Efforts for Edo built burial goods," ''Asahi Shimbun'' (February 8, 2023).</ref>
== area == The area of the mound are as follows:<ref name="OhtawaraCity"/><ref name="descriptionboard"/> * '''Mound length:''' 114 meters * '''Posterior section:''' ** Length: 60.5 meters ** Width: 58 meters ** Height: 11.5 meters * '''Anterior section:''' ** Length: 53.5 meters ** Width: 52 meters ** Height: 6.5 meters
The surrounding moat is visible on the north, west, and south sides, while the east side features a natural slope leading to the Naka River.
== Historical Significance == Kamisamuraizuka Kofun, along with Shimosamuraizuka Kofun, is celebrated as the site of Japan's first academically documented archaeological excavation. Under Tokugawa Mitsukuni's orders, the excavation aimed to explore and preserve the cultural heritage of the Nasu region. The discovery of burial goods, followed by their careful documentation and reburial, marks a significant milestone in Japanese archaeology and cultural preservation.<ref name="Asahi20230208"/>
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site in 1951 as part of the "Samuraizuka Kofun" complex.<ref name="NationalHistoric Site">{{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}.</ref>
== Related Sites == * '''Shimosamuraizuka Kofun:''' Located nearby, this zenpō-kōhō-fun complements Kamisamuraizuka in historical and archaeological importance. * '''Kamisamuraizuka North Kofun:''' A smaller zenpō-kōhō-fun situated north of Kamisamuraizuka. Little is known about this kofun due to the lack of detailed investigations.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/115806 "Kamisamuraizuka Kofun Excavation Reports"] (Tochigi Future Creation Foundation, 2021). * [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BE%8D%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3-69803 "Samuraizuka Kofun"] - Kotobank entry.
== External Links == * {{Cite database|title=Samuraizuka Kofun|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/401/483|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} * [https://www.city.ohtawara.tochigi.jp/docs/2013082767660/ Samuraizuka Kofun] - Ōtawara City website.
{{Kofun navbar}}
